source: north-budget.pdf (English and Kannada Speech - Bengaluru North City Corporation, OpenCity.in)
extraction_notes: PDF is bilingual. Kannada pages 1-49 (English appears starting page 3 as a cover; English body runs pages 50-77; summary tables on pages 78-81).
Source text extracted via PyMuPDF (pdfplumber also extracts text cleanly - no CID glyphs). English section is the primary source; Kannada provided via fitz and cross-checked where amounts differed.

Speech is structured as 147 numbered items organised under ~14 section headings (Revenue, Finance and Accounts, Advertisement, Land Acquisition & E-DRC/E-TDR, Town Planning, Administration, Welfare, Public Relation, Sanitation, Forest, Horticulture, Health, Health Department (Clinical), Animal Husbandry, Public Works, Lakes, Education).
Amounts quoted in crore (1 crore = 100 lakh) are converted to lakhs here for consistency.

Some items are revenue targets (property tax, advertisement, Premium FAR, land acquisition, B-to-A Khata conversion) rather than expenditures; captured and tagged with category 'other' where relevant.
Items without a specific named scheme or quoted amount have been omitted per spec (e.g. general 'improve parks' statements). Only concrete named initiatives or specific quoted amounts are included.
Page numbers below refer to English-section speech pages (50-77). Kannada-section page numbers for the same items run roughly pages 4-48.

budget_totals_for_reference.note: All figures from 'Source of Income' and 'Payment Classification' tables on English pages 78 and 80; totals match the 'Budget bird eye-view' narrative on page 76 (₹4,342.56 crore receipts, ₹4,342.40 crore expenditure, ₹1.61 crore surplus).

projects.name: Property tax collection (2026-27 target)
projects.description: Expected property tax revenue of Rs.826.25 crore in 2026-27 from the 7 assembly constituencies under BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: The Bengaluru North City Corporation covers 7 assembly constituencies, and for the fiscal year 2026-27, property tax revenue of ₹826.25 crore is expected.

projects.name: Stamp duty (mutation applications) revenue
projects.description: From currently active mutation applications, about 2% from stamp duty is expected to yield Rs.40 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: from currently active mutation applications, about 2% from Stamp Duty is expected to yield ₹40.00 crore, and higher revenue is expected from new account approval applications.

projects.name: PSU and development charges under single plots
projects.description: From PSUs and development charges under single plots, a total of Rs.97.15 crore is expected.
projects.verbatim_quote: In the fiscal year 2026-27, a total of ₹97.15 crore is expected from PSUs and development charges under single plots.

projects.name: Property tax revision revenue
projects.description: Additional revenue from property tax revision expected at Rs.30.87 crore; combined property tax / income target is Rs.128.02 crore.
projects.verbatim_quote: From the revision of property tax, ₹30.87 crore is expected. Including all the above factors, a total property tax/income target of approximately ₹128.02 crore has been set.

projects.name: GIS-based e-account property tax system
projects.description: Implement a Geographic Information System (GIS) based e-account property tax system.
projects.verbatim_quote: A Geographic Information System (GIS)-based e-account property tax system will be implemented.

projects.name: B-Register to A-Khatha property conversion
projects.description: Conversion of properties registered in B-Register into A-Khatha; expected revenue of approximately Rs.680 crore in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: The process of converting properties registered in B-Register into A-Khatha properties is underway, and for 2026-27, revenue of approximately ₹680 crore is expected.

projects.name: Digitisation of property records
projects.description: Steps will be taken to digitize property records within the BNCC jurisdiction.
projects.verbatim_quote: Steps will be taken to digitize property records within the Bengaluru North City Corporation jurisdiction.

projects.name: GPS/drone property tax revision for buildings
projects.description: Additional revenue expected from property tax revision of buildings using GPS and drone imagery.
projects.verbatim_quote: Additional revenue is expected from property tax revision of buildings in Bengaluru North City Corporation using GPS and drone imagery.

projects.name: E-Khatha software for automatic account transfers
projects.description: E-Khatha software developed to assist automatic account transfers; online access to services for citizens.
projects.verbatim_quote: E-Khatha software has been developed to assist in automatic account transfers. This will enable citizens to access services online conveniently.

projects.name: Dishank-style GPS property identification
projects.description: Following the Revenue Department's Dishank application, GPS-based access will be provided to citizens in North Bengaluru to identify properties quickly.
projects.verbatim_quote: Following the example of the Dishank application of the Revenue Department, steps have been taken to provide Geographic Information System (GPS) access to citizens in North Bengaluru, helping them quickly identify their properties.

projects.name: Online payments and host-to-host bank integration
projects.description: Manage all revenue and payments online with host-to-host bank integration and real-time transaction recording.
projects.verbatim_quote: various receipts of the corporation have already been integrated with banks through host-to-host coordination, and receipts are being processed online with real-time recording of transactions.

projects.name: Direct Benefit Transfers for welfare beneficiaries
projects.description: Direct Benefit Transfers planned for eligible beneficiaries of welfare schemes within BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: Direct Benefit Transfers are planned for eligible beneficiaries of welfare schemes within Bengaluru North City Corporation.

projects.name: Audit Cell for objections and recoveries
projects.description: Establish an Audit Cell to take prompt action on objections and recoveries from audit observations.
projects.verbatim_quote: An Audit Cell will be established to take prompt action regarding objections and recoveries from audit observations.

projects.name: 15th Central Finance Commission grant
projects.description: Expected grant of Rs.25 crore from the 15th Finance Commission.
projects.verbatim_quote: Bengaluru North City Corporation expects grants of ₹25.00 crores from the 15th Finance Commission.

projects.name: 16th Central Finance Commission grant
projects.description: Expected grant of Rs.128.29 crore from the 16th Finance Commission.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹128.29 crore from the 16th Finance Commission

projects.name: Special Basic Infrastructure grant
projects.description: Expected grant of Rs.429.27 crore from special basic infrastructure grants.
projects.verbatim_quote: and ₹429.27 crore from special basic infrastructure grants.

projects.name: Greater Bengaluru (Advertisement) Rules-2025 implementation
projects.description: Implementation of Greater Bengaluru Area (Advertisement) Rules-2025 for transparent, regulated commercial and self-ad installation; public auction (tender) mechanism; One-Time Opportunity (OTO) for converting permitted self-ads to commercial boards.
projects.verbatim_quote: To ensure transparent and regulated installation of commercial and self-ads within the corporation limits, the Greater Bengaluru Area (Advertisement) Rules-2025 came into effect in 2025-26.

projects.name: New advertisement tender process (license fees)
projects.description: New advertisement tender process; approximately Rs.1.25 crore expected from license fees.
projects.verbatim_quote: A new advertisement tender process is underway within Bengaluru North City Corporation, and it is expected to collect approximately ₹1.25 crore in license fees.

projects.name: Unauthorised advertisements penalty and criminal action
projects.description: Penalty of twice the tender amount and criminal cases permitted against unauthorised ad installers under Greater Bengaluru Advertisement Rules-2025.
projects.verbatim_quote: To control unauthorized advertisements, the rules stipulate payment of a penalty of twice the tendered amount and allow for filing criminal cases.

projects.name: Advertisement revenue target
projects.description: Expected advertisement revenue of approximately Rs.108 crore in 2026-27 under new advertisement rules.
projects.verbatim_quote: Bengaluru North City Corporation expects to collect approximately ₹108 crore in advertisement revenue in the fiscal year 2026-27.

projects.name: Land acquisition for municipal infrastructure
projects.description: Rs.17 crore allocated for land acquisition to ensure timely availability of land for municipal infrastructure projects.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 17.00 crore has been allocated for land acquisition in this financial year.

projects.name: E-DRC / E-TDR digitisation and online marketplace
projects.description: Digitise E-DRC/E-TDR to facilitate transparent distribution, online exchange, and free public participation in the online marketplace.
projects.verbatim_quote: Steps have been taken to digitize E-DRC/E-TDR to facilitate transparent distribution... enabling online sale and purchase of E-DRC/E-TDR.

projects.name: Town Planning fee revenue (building plans, OC, FAR)
projects.description: Expected revenue of Rs.817.37 crore from building plan approvals, plinth/initial certificates, occupancy certificates, premium FAR fees, 5% parks/open spaces fees, and other sources.
projects.verbatim_quote: an estimated revenue of Rs. 817.37 crore is expected in 2026-27 from building plan approvals, plinth/initial certificates, occupancy certificates, premium F.A.R fees, 5% fees on parks/open spaces in single plot plan approvals, and other sources.

projects.name: Vacant municipal properties single-plot applications
projects.description: Applications for vacant municipal properties through single plots; expected revenue of Rs.100 crore in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: applications for vacant municipal properties within the corporation's jurisdiction will be accepted through single plots, with an expected revenue of Rs. 100.00 crore.

projects.name: Urban Design Cell city map (maintenance)
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh allocated for maintenance of the city map created by the Urban Design Cell.
projects.verbatim_quote: The Urban Design Cell has created a city map for the corporation's jurisdiction, with Rs. 50.00 lakh allocated for maintenance.

projects.name: Greater Bengaluru Comprehensive Master Plan contribution
projects.description: Rs.5 crore allocated by BNCC towards the master plan being tendered by the Greater Bangalore Authority.
projects.verbatim_quote: For the preparation of this master plan, Rs. 5.00 crore has been allocated by the Bangalore North City Corporation.

projects.name: Online Building Plan System (OBPS) / Auto DCR
projects.description: Auto DCR software and Online Building Plan System (OBPS) for simplified, transparent building plan approvals under Ease of Doing Business.
projects.verbatim_quote: Auto DCR Software has been developed to provide approvals through the Online Building Plan System (OBPS), and currently, approvals are being issued entirely online.

projects.name: Inter-department clearance integration in OBPS
projects.description: Integrate the process of obtaining clearance letters from internal and external departments within the OBPS software.
projects.verbatim_quote: It is intended to integrate the process of obtaining clearance letters from internal and external departments within the software.

projects.name: Nambike Nakshe auto-approval for small residential buildings
projects.description: Under the citizen-friendly Nambike Nakshe project, building plan approvals for four-unit residential buildings on plots up to 375 sq.m granted automatically within 15 days.
projects.verbatim_quote: building plan approvals for four-unit residential buildings on plots up to 375 sq. m are being granted automatically within 15 days through the Nambike Nakshe software.

projects.name: TDR and Premium FAR permissions
projects.description: Permissions granted using TDR and Premium FAR with additional proportional area; Premium FAR fees calculated and collected per High Court order.
projects.verbatim_quote: Following the High Court's order, permissions are being granted using TDR and Premium FAR with additional proportional area, and Premium FAR fees are calculated and collected in such cases.

projects.name: Staff count scientific review and increase proposal
projects.description: Proposal submitted to Government to scientifically review and increase staff numbers and hire new personnel commensurate with Bangalore's growing population.
projects.verbatim_quote: a proposal has been submitted to the government to scientifically review and increase staff numbers and hire new personnel to ensure timely and satisfactory services to citizens.

projects.name: E-Office rollout across all BNCC offices
projects.description: E-Office currently operational; to be rapidly implemented across all BNCC offices.
projects.verbatim_quote: E-Office is currently operational in the Bangalore North City Corporation and will be rapidly implemented across all offices.

projects.name: Files and records digitisation
projects.description: Files and records within BNCC jurisdiction will be digitized.
projects.verbatim_quote: Files and records within the Bangalore North City Corporation jurisdiction will be digitized.

projects.name: Staff technical and professional training programme
projects.description: Technical and professional training programmes to enhance efficiency of BNCC officers and staff.
projects.verbatim_quote: Measures will be taken to provide technical and professional training to enhance the efficiency of corporation officers and staff.

projects.name: Biometric attendance management system
projects.description: Biometric attendance system to be implemented for officers and staff attendance.
projects.verbatim_quote: A biometric attendance system will be implemented for the attendance of officers and staff.

projects.name: BNCC own central office building
projects.description: Rs.40 crore allocated to construct a new central office building for the newly established BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 40.00 crore has been allocated for constructing a new central office building.

projects.name: North City Corporation office on 4th floor of Yelahanka Mini Vidhana Soudha
projects.description: Rs.10 crore allocated to build the BNCC office complex on the 4th floor of the Yelahanka Mini Vidhana Soudha building.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 10.00 crore has been allocated to construct the North City Corporation office complex on the 4th floor of the Yelahanka Mini Vidhana Soudha building.

projects.name: Administration software development
projects.description: Rs.6 crore allocated to develop software to facilitate the administration of BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 6.00 crore has been allocated to develop software that will facilitate the administration of Bangalore North City Corporation.

projects.name: Regularisation of D.P.S. Pourakarmikas and salary allocation

projects.verbatim_quote: a total of 2,767 D.P.S municipal workers were regularized for worker welfare, and Rs. 150.00 crore has been allocated for the salaries of municipal workers in 2026-27.

projects.name: Education welfare (tuition/registration/exam fee reimbursement + overseas aid)
projects.description: Rs.8.94 crore allocated for educational programs; reimburse fees for ~1,700 students (Class 1 to postgraduate) and provide financial aid for overseas education.
projects.verbatim_quote: In the 2026–27 budget, a total of ₹8.94 crore has been allocated for educational programs... reimburse tuition fees, registration fees, and examination fees paid by approximately 1,700 students.

projects.verbatim_quote: Under the e-Sarathi scheme for the year 2026–27, ₹4.08 crore has been allocated... to around 350 eligible beneficiaries.

projects.description: Rs.71.07 crore for housing; 50% to single-house scheme and 50% via Rajiv Gandhi Housing Corporation; subsidy up to Rs.5 lakh per beneficiary for ~1,420 beneficiaries.
projects.verbatim_quote: For the housing scheme in the 2026–27 budget, ₹71.07 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: Health programs for all sections (serious illness aid)
projects.description: Rs.11.12 crore for health programs; medical assistance for serious and life-threatening diseases (heart disease, kidney failure, cancer).
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the health programs for all sections in 2026–27, a total of ₹11.12 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: Electric two-wheelers for working women and civic workers
projects.description: Rs.1 crore to distribute electric two-wheelers to ~100 eligible working women and civic workers (Brand Bengaluru).
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Brand Bengaluru concept, ₹1.00 crore has been reserved this year to distribute electric two-wheelers to eligible working women and civic workers.

projects.name: Pourakarmika welfare (laptops, daughter's marriage aid, skill training)
projects.description: Rs.10 crore for civic worker welfare: laptops for children pursuing higher education, up to Rs.2 lakh marriage aid for daughters, skill training via Skill Development Department.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹10.00 crore has been allocated for the welfare of civic workers... distributing laptops to children... up to ₹2 lakh to reduce the burden of simple marriage expenses for daughters.

projects.name: Free laptops for SC/ST/backward/minority degree students
projects.description: Rs.7.60 crore to distribute free laptops to ~1,200 degree students from SC/ST and backward/minority communities.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹7.60 crore has been allocated to distribute free laptops to students from Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, backward and minority communities pursuing degree education.

projects.name: Tricycles for specially-abled persons
projects.description: Rs.1.28 crore for distributing tricycles to specially-abled persons.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹1.28 crore has been allocated for distributing tricycles to specially-abled persons.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹73 lakh has been allocated for the welfare of the transgender community in 2026–27. The objective is to provide one-time financial assistance of up to ₹50,000.

projects.description: Rs.1.27 crore for women's welfare: sewing machines for ~400 tailoring-trained women, Rs.50 lakh for electric two-wheelers, Rs.27 lakh for Akka Cafe (Cafe Sanjeevini) under National Livelihood Mission.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹1.27 crore has been allocated for women's welfare programs in 2026–27... 'Akka Cafe,' aimed at providing clean, tasty, and affordable local food.

projects.description: Rs.1.47 crore for senior citizen welfare programs.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹1.47 crore has been allocated for senior citizen welfare programs.

projects.name: Street vendor vending zones
projects.description: Rs.60 lakh to establish vending zones under the welfare program for street vendors.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹60 lakh has been allocated in the 2026–27 budget to establish vending zones under the welfare program for street vendors.

projects.description: Rs.7.10 crore to provide e-vending vehicles to street vendors; economic and social strengthening.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹7.10 crore has been allocated to provide e-vending vehicles to street vendors within the corporation limits.

projects.description: Rs.1.10 crore for maintenance of night shelters for the homeless.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹1.10 crore has been allocated in the 2026–27 budget for the maintenance of night shelters for the homeless.

projects.name: General category welfare programs

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹5.60 crore has been allocated for general category welfare programs.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹1 crore has been allocated to provide financial assistance to athletes participating in national and international sports.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹50 lakh has been allocated under the Honorable Mayor's discretionary fund to provide medical relief facilities for journalists and media reporters.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹22 lakh has been reserved for a group insurance scheme for small and medium journalists/reporters.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹2 crore has been allocated under the Honorable Mayor's discretionary fund to provide medical relief facilities.

projects.verbatim_quote: ₹2 crore has been allocated under the discretionary fund of corporation members for providing medical relief facilities.

projects.name: Slum area development (NOC to Slum Clearance Board)
projects.description: Issue no-objection certificates to the Slum Clearance Board without levying charges; relocate slums in lake encroachment areas using Slum Cess and SCP/TSP funds.
projects.verbatim_quote: For the development of slum areas within the corporation limits, cooperation will be extended to the Slum Clearance Board.

projects.description: A separate website will be developed for the Bangalore North City Corporation.
projects.verbatim_quote: A separate website will be developed for the Bangalore North City Corporation.

projects.name: Social media citizen outreach (Facebook, Instagram, YouTube)
projects.description: Facebook, Instagram and YouTube accounts managed to inform citizens about all-department development activities.
projects.verbatim_quote: Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube social media accounts are being managed to provide information to the public about development activities.

projects.name: Phone-in Solution-On program (every Friday)
projects.description: Innovative phone-in Solution-On program every Friday for receiving and resolving citizen complaints immediately.
projects.verbatim_quote: the corporation is implementing an innovative model of a phone-in 'solution on' program every Friday.

projects.name: 24/7 complaint recording system
projects.description: A 24/7 system for recording public complaints and forwarding them to concerned officials; to be strengthened further.
projects.verbatim_quote: A 24/7 system is in place to record complaints from the public and forward them to the concerned officials, which will be further strengthened.

projects.name: Daily ward sanitation + door-to-door waste collection
projects.description: Daily sanitation of all ward roads; 3,052 municipal workers, 988 auto tippers, 110 compactors for door-to-door collection.
projects.verbatim_quote: Daily sanitation of all wards roads in Bangalore North City Corporation is carried out by municipal workers, and door-to-door waste collection is done using auto tippers.

projects.name: Mission Clean Airport Road
projects.description: Clean around 34 km of Bellary Road leading to the international airport.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the title 'Mission Clean Airport Road,' a program has been conducted to clean around 34 kilometres of the Bellary Road leading to the international airport.

projects.name: Dasarahalli farmer waste processing initiative (600t)
projects.description: 50 farmers identified in Dasarahalli division; approximately 600 tons of waste transported from their lands for processing.
projects.verbatim_quote: As an innovative effort from the Dasarahalli division of North City Corporation, 50 farmers have been identified, and approximately 600 tons of waste from their lands will be transported for processing.

projects.name: Hebbal division green-waste compost
projects.description: In Hebbal division, green waste converted into compost priced at Rs.150 per kg; 300 kg sold.
projects.verbatim_quote: In the Hebbal division of North City Corporation, green waste will be converted into compost, priced at Rs. 150 per kg.

projects.name: 110 CCTVs at waste-dumping black spots

projects.verbatim_quote: In the North City Corporation area, locations have been identified to install a total of 110 CCTVs to identify individuals dumping waste in black spot areas.

projects.name: Littering and single-use plastic fine enforcement

projects.verbatim_quote: From 01-09-2025 to 31-01-2026 in the North City Corporation area, fines of Rs. 28 lakhs will be collected for 3,019 cases of littering.

projects.name: Pourakarmika benefits (festival advance, leave encashment, DA, jayanti)
projects.description: Rs.19.12 crore for permanent municipal workers' festival advances, 21-day leave, 15-day earned leave, pending DA, 300-day retirement/death leave cash settlements, death relief, Ambedkar and Kempegowda Jayanti, and salary increments.
projects.verbatim_quote: a total of Rs. 19.12 crore has been allocated for festival advances, 21-day leave periods, 15-day earned leave, pending DA amounts.

projects.name: DPS Pourakarmika salaries
projects.description: Rs.13 crore allocated for 2026-27 for DPS municipal workers performing duties in BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 13.00 crore has been allocated for the 2026-27 fiscal year.

projects.name: Pourakarmika PPE (gloves, masks, safety equipment)
projects.description: Gloves, masks and equipment provided for health check-ups and safety of municipal workers.
projects.verbatim_quote: Measures have been taken to provide gloves, masks, and other equipment for the health check-up and safety of municipal workers.

projects.name: BSWML administrative + O&M + capital allocation
projects.description: Rs.13.38 crore administrative, Rs.129.57 crore solid-waste collection/transport/management, and Rs.157.05 crore capital works to Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited for 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: for administrative expenses ₹13.38 crore, for solid waste collection–transport stations and other management ₹129.57 crore, and for capital works ₹157.05 crore have been allocated to Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited.

projects.name: Sweeping machines for main roads
projects.description: Rs.12 crore earmarked for the purchase of sweeping machines to keep main roads clean.
projects.verbatim_quote: a total amount of ₹12.00 crore has been earmarked for the purchase of sweeping machines.

projects.name: Human-assisted garbage collection / litter-picking machines
projects.description: Rs.5 crore allocated to purchase human-assisted garbage collection / litter-picking machines to simplify sanitation work.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹5.00 crore has been allocated by the corporation for the purchase of human-assisted garbage collection machines / litter-picking machines.

projects.name: Pulakeshinagar division animal waste management
projects.description: Rs.2.70 crore allocated for animal waste management in Pulakeshinagar division for 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: For animal waste management in the Pulakeshinagar division, a total of Rs. 2.70 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: Solid waste unit and machinery repair & maintenance
projects.description: Rs.10 lakh for repair and maintenance of solid waste units and machinery; Rs.33 lakh for other maintenance.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 10.00 lakh has been allocated for repair and maintenance of solid waste units and machinery, and Rs. 33.00 lakh for other maintenance.

projects.name: Ward mastering for SWM
projects.description: Rs.94.50 lakh allocated for ward mastering in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 94.50 lakh has been allocated for ward mastering in the 2026-27 fiscal year.

projects.name: SWM Information-Education-Communication (IEC)
projects.description: Rs.98.50 lakh allocated for information, education and communication for 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 98.50 lakh has been allocated for information, education, and communication for the 2026-27 fiscal year.

projects.verbatim_quote: It is planned to construct Transfer Stations at eight locations for waste management in the Bangalore North City Corporation area.

projects.description: Steps to provide auto-rickshaw driving training to women municipal workers.
projects.verbatim_quote: Steps will be taken to provide training for women municipal workers to drive auto rickshaws.

projects.verbatim_quote: a total grant of ₹1051.00 lakh has been allocated under the slogan 'Green is Life' for the comprehensive management of trees, wildlife conservation, and forest activities.

projects.verbatim_quote: a grant of ₹30.00 lakh has been allocated for this census work.

projects.verbatim_quote: a grant of ₹500.00 lakh has been allocated.

projects.verbatim_quote: a grant of ₹416.00 lakh has been allocated for planting and nurturing trees.

projects.verbatim_quote: For wildlife protection, including capturing and conserving poisonous animals like snakes and relocating them to appropriate habitats, a grant of ₹15.00 lakh has been allocated.

projects.verbatim_quote: In Bengaluru North City Corporation, there are over 336 parks and 38 road medians. For their proper maintenance, a grant of ₹37.00 crore has been allocated.

projects.description: Public toilets already in 60 parks; 50 more planned for 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: This year, it is planned to construct toilets in 50 more parks for greater public convenience.

projects.name: Rodent control activities
projects.description: Rs.8.86 crore allocated for rodent control activities in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: An amount of Rs. 8.86 crore has been allocated for rodent control activities in the year 2026-27.

projects.name: 100-bed hospitals in Sarvagnanagar & Byatarayanapura
projects.description: Construction of 100-bed hospital buildings in Sarvagnanagar and Byatarayanapura assembly constituencies; Karnataka Government coordinating completion and equipment.
projects.verbatim_quote: A 100-bed capacity hospital buildings is being constructed in the jurisdiction of Sarvagnanagar and Byatarayanapura Assembly constituencies.

projects.name: Free dialysis services (Ganganagar, Sanjayanagar, Mallasandra)
projects.description: Free dialysis at Ganganagar Heritage Hospital; new machines at Sanjayanagar PHC and Mallasandra Dialysis Center; Rs.50 lakh for management.
projects.verbatim_quote: Free dialysis services are being provided at Ganganagar Heritage Hospital... An amount of Rs. 50.00 lakh has been allocated for the management of dialysis centers.

projects.name: DJ Halli & Munireddipalya Heritage Hospitals merger and upgrade
projects.description: Rs.35 crore for merging DJ Halli and Munireddipalya Heritage Hospitals; upgrade building works and equip primary health centers under Brand Bangalore.
projects.verbatim_quote: with Rs. 35.00 crore allocated to upgrade the necessary building works and equip the primary health centers with instruments and equipment.

projects.name: Indira Canteens operation (daily wage workers, seniors, students)
projects.description: Rs.11.15 crore allocated for Indira Canteens providing healthy, affordable meals (ragi mudde 3 days/week).
projects.verbatim_quote: An amount of Rs. 11.15 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: M.R. Palya and D.J. Halli maternity hospital -> 30-bed CHCs (IPHS 2022)
projects.description: Convert maternity hospitals at M.R. Palya and D.J. Halli into 30-bed Community Health Centres under IPHS 2022 (Brand Bengaluru).
projects.verbatim_quote: the maternity hospitals at M.R. Palya and D.J. Halli will be converted into Community Health Centres (CHCs) with a capacity of 30 beds each.

projects.name: CHC specialist services (surgery, dental, paediatrics, emergency)
projects.description: New CHCs to provide general surgery, dental care, physician services, obstetrics/gynaecology, paediatrics, emergency and ambulance services.
projects.verbatim_quote: These hospitals will provide services including general surgery, dental care, physician services, obstetrics and gynaecology, paediatrics, as well as emergency and ambulance services.

projects.name: Namma Clinic / UPHC repairs, physiotherapy, dental
projects.description: Rs.35 crore for repair works in Namma Clinics and UPHCs; essential equipment; new physiotherapy centres and dental clinics.
projects.verbatim_quote: A total amount of ₹35.00 crore has been allocated to Bengaluru North City Corporation for the implementation of these works.

projects.name: 100-bed multispeciality hospitals operationalisation (Sarvagnanagar, Byatarayanapura, Pulikeshi Nagar)
projects.description: Construction of 100-bed hospitals complete in Sarvagnanagar, Byatarayanapura, Pulikeshi Nagar; operationalisation in coordination with Department of Health and Family Welfare.
projects.verbatim_quote: The construction of 100-bedded hospitals in Sarvagnanagar, Byatarayanapura, and Pulikeshi Nagar Assembly Constituencies has been completed.

projects.name: Trade license simplification (Ease of Doing Business)
projects.description: Simplified trade license procedures; online issuance; technological integration to bring unauthorized commercial establishments under licensing.
projects.verbatim_quote: To reduce delays in obtaining trade licenses and to improve the Ease of Doing Business, procedures have been simplified.

projects.name: Street dog control, rabies eradication, pet-ownership awareness
projects.description: Key animal welfare programs implemented for street dog control, rabies eradication, and responsible pet ownership awareness.
projects.verbatim_quote: Steps are being taken for street dog control, rabies eradication, and raising public awareness about responsible pet ownership.

projects.name: Modern slaughterhouse establishment (initial allocation)
projects.description: Rs.14 crore for animal welfare and slaughterhouse programs.
projects.verbatim_quote: Establishing a modern slaughterhouse to ensure a healthy supply of meat products is a primary goal of this department. Rs. 14.00 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: ABC (Animal Birth Control) surgery for 12,000 street dogs
projects.description: Rs.3 crore to perform ABC surgery on 12,000 street dogs covering medicine, surgical supplies, medical staff, food and post-operative care.
projects.verbatim_quote: In 2026–27, a target has been set to perform ABC surgery on 12,000 street dogs. Rs. 3.00 crore has been allocated.

projects.name: Mass Dog Vaccination (65,000 dogs, rabies + combined)
projects.description: Rs.5 crore for Mass Dog Vaccination program targeting approximately 65,000 dogs in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: approximately 65,000 dogs are targeted to receive rabies and combined vaccines. Rs. 5.00 crore has been earmarked.

projects.name: Animal welfare Information-Education-Communication
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh for IEC programs on animal welfare and public health.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 50.00 lakh has been allocated to effectively implement these programs.

projects.name: Modern slaughterhouse project (phased)
projects.description: Rs.10 crore for hygienic slaughter line, veterinary inspection, waste processing, refrigeration, environmentally-friendly waste management; phased implementation.
projects.verbatim_quote: The estimated cost of this project... is Rs. 10.00 crore. The project will be implemented in phases.

projects.name: Dog shelter for 500 dogs (AWBI/PCA 1960 compliant)
projects.description: Rs.3.80 crore for dog shelter facilities for ~500 dogs including treatment, surgical facilities, quarantine ward, food, care; animal crematorium steps.
projects.verbatim_quote: Facilities for about 500 dogs, including shelter, treatment, surgical facilities, quarantine ward, food, and care, will be provided, with Rs. 3.80 crore allocated.

projects.name: Street dog management measures (citizen safety)
projects.description: Rs.1.50 crore allocated for several measures to facilitate street dog management and ensure citizen safety.
projects.verbatim_quote: with Rs. 1.50 crore allocated for this purpose.

projects.name: Public Works revenue (community hall rentals, OFC, permits)
projects.description: Rs.32 crore expected from community hall rentals, building rentals, OFC, permits, road cutting, and other sources in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: income of Rs. 32.00 crore is expected from community hall rentals, building rentals, OFC, permits, road cutting, and other sources.

projects.name: On-street and off-street parking revenue
projects.description: Rs.6 crore expected from on-street and off-street vehicle parking projects in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: regarding vehicle parking, income of Rs. 6.00 crore is expected through on-street and off-street parking projects.

projects.name: Special infrastructure projects (Central + CM's basic infrastructure)
projects.description: Rs.935.56 crore allocated for 2026-27 for Central Government and Honourable Chief Minister's basic infrastructure development projects.
projects.verbatim_quote: Special infrastructure projects from the Central Government, related to Honourable Chief Ministers' basic infrastructure development projects and other plans, have been allocated Rs. 935.56 crore.

projects.name: Dilapidated ward roads (CM basic infrastructure plan - 197.90 km)
projects.description: Rs.248 crore for 2025-26 under CM's basic infrastructure plan for development of 197.90 km ward roads in BNCC.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 248 crores has been allocated for the development of 197.90 km of ward roads under the Honourable Chief Ministers' basic infrastructure development plan.

projects.name: Capital Support Plan (187.03 km main/sub-main roads)
projects.description: Rs.248.71 crore under Capital Support Plan for Special Basic Infrastructure to develop 187.03 km of main and sub-main roads.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 248.71 crore under the Capital Support Plan for Special Basic Infrastructure to develop 187.03 km of main and sub-main roads.

projects.name: Arterial/sub-arterial roads (118.29 km, Re-ESROW)
projects.description: Rs.176.50 crore for development of 118.29 km of arterial and sub-arterial roads under Re-ESROW Grants (total project Rs.694 crore).
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 176.50 crore is allocated for developing 118.29 km of arterial and sub-arterial roads under re-ESROW Grants.

projects.name: Ward maintenance allocation (Rs.2.25 crore/ward)
projects.description: Per-ward maintenance: Rs.20 lakh desilting drains, Rs.10 lakh potholes, Rs.25 lakh footpath, Rs.20 lakh traffic works (Rs.75 lakh/ward) + Rs.1.50 crore/ward development works = Rs.2.25 crore/ward across 72 wards.
projects.verbatim_quote: totaling ₹75 lakhs per ward. Additionally, ₹1.50 crores per ward have been earmarked for various development works including ward-level roads and major road repairs and maintenance, making a total allocation of ₹2.25 crores per ward.

projects.name: Pedestrian path repair & reconstruction (Rs.25 lakh/ward)
projects.description: Rs.25 lakh per ward (Rs.18 crore total) for pedestrian path repair and reconstruction; 1,000 km well-structured paths.
projects.verbatim_quote: A grant of Rs. 25 lakh per ward is allocated in the municipality area, totalling Rs. 18.00 crore, for pedestrian path repair and reconstruction.

projects.name: Pedestrian junction development + skywalks
projects.description: Rs.50 crore for development of pedestrian path junctions, construction of skywalks, and related structures.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 50.00 crore is allocated for the development of pedestrian path junctions, construction of skywalks, and related structures.

projects.name: Stormwater drain repair & maintenance
projects.description: Rs.10 crore for repair and maintenance of stormwater drains in BNCC area for flood control.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 10.00 crore is allocated for the repair and maintenance of stormwater drains.

projects.name: Flood control - 53.42 km concrete retaining walls (World Bank/NDMF)
projects.description: Rs.532.52 crore World Bank/NDMF funded project for 53.42 km box/U-shaped concrete retaining walls for stormwater drains; completion in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: the construction of 53.42 km of concrete retaining walls (box and U-shaped) for stormwater drains, undertaken in 2025-26 with World Bank and NDMF funding totalling Rs. 532.52 crore.

projects.name: Streetlight repair and electrical works
projects.description: Rs.9.40 crore for repair/maintenance of streetlights, equipment, and electrical works in BNCC buildings, playgrounds, parks, community halls, electrical substations.
projects.verbatim_quote: Rs. 9.40 crore is allocated for the repair and maintenance of streetlights and related equipment.

projects.name: LED streetlight PPP conversion
projects.description: 124,142 streetlights being converted to LED under PPP; 49,767 already installed (76.93%); remaining 23.07% in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: The North City Municipality area has 124,142 streetlights, of which 49767 LED streetlights have already been installed, completing 76.93% of the work.

projects.name: Legislative assembly constituency development grant
projects.description: Rs.120 crore for various development works in each legislative assembly constituency within BNCC in 2026-27.
projects.verbatim_quote: A grant of Rs. 120.00 crore is provided in 2026-27 for various development works in each legislative assembly constituency.

projects.name: Road widening under RMP-2015 (12 roads, 30.5 km)
projects.description: Rs.25 crore in 2026-27 for new road widening of 4 roads (7.95 km): Nitte Meenakshi Engineering College Road, Gas Pipeline Road, Dasarahalli Main Road, Hennur-Bagalur Road; also 22.5 km on 8 BBMP-initiated roads to complete.
projects.verbatim_quote: A budget allocation of ₹25.00 crores has been earmarked for the year 2026–27 for the new road widening works.

projects.name: Community Involvement for Lake Conservation (CICL) 2024
projects.description: Under CICL-2024 policy, actions to involve citizens and interested private organisations to provide facilities in lakes.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the 'Community Involvement for Lake Conservation Policy (CICL)-2024,' actions will be taken involving citizens and interested private organizations to provide various facilities in lakes.

projects.name: Annual maintenance of 30 developed lakes
projects.description: Rs.8 crore for annual maintenance of 30 developed lakes; Home Guards deployed; plastic-free zones declared.
projects.verbatim_quote: An allocation of ₹8.00 crores has been set aside for the annual maintenance of 30 developed lakes.

projects.name: Lake development (Srinivasapura, Mallasandra Gudde, others)
projects.description: Rs.40 crore for development of remaining lakes in 2026-27; 36 living lakes total, 30 already developed, Hebbal and Nagawara in progress.
projects.verbatim_quote: For the remaining lakes to be developed, ₹40.00 crores will be allocated in 2026-27.

projects.name: Singapore Lake, Banaswadi, Lingarajapura, Battaguntepalya, Ramachandrapura Lake revival
projects.description: Comprehensive development of Singapore Lake and revival of non-functional lakes (Banaswadi, Lingarajapura, Battaguntepalya, Ramachandrapura) via extensive project report.
projects.verbatim_quote: Comprehensive development of Singapore Lake in the Greater North Bangalore Municipal area, as well as the revival of non-functional lakes such as Banaswadi Lake, Lingarajapura Lake, Battaguntepalya Lake, and Ramachandrapura Lake.

projects.name: Climate action plan works
projects.description: Rs.20 crore for new works under the climate action plan to prevent water, air and other pollution.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹20.00 crores will be allocated for new works related to the climate action plan within the municipal area.

projects.name: Boating facilities in lakes
projects.description: Measures to implement boating facilities in lakes under the municipal jurisdiction.
projects.verbatim_quote: Measures will be taken to implement boating facilities in the lakes under the municipal jurisdiction.

projects.name: Free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes, sweaters
projects.description: Rs.4 crore for free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes & socks, sweaters for students in BNCC schools/colleges.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹4 crores have been allocated to provide enrolled students in schools/colleges with free textbooks, notebooks, uniforms, bags, shoes and socks, and sweaters.

projects.name: Vinayakanagar / M.S. Palya new school-college complex (furniture and equipment)
projects.description: Rs.2 crore for furniture and equipment for new primary school, high school and pre-university college in Vinayakanagar, M.S. Palya (Battarahalli Assembly constituency).
projects.verbatim_quote: In Vinayakanagar, M.S. Palya... ₹2 crores allocated for necessary furniture and equipment.

projects.name: SSLC/PUC/degree top-performer incentive funds
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh incentive: Rs.25,000 for top SSLC performers, Rs.35,000 for second PUC, Rs.50,000 for final degree highest distinction.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹50 lakhs have been allocated as incentive funds.

projects.name: Holistic student development (sports, Scouts, DIET, IIT)
projects.description: Rs.47 lakh for inter-school/college sports & cultural competitions, Bharat Scouts and Guides training, subject-specific DIET/IIT teacher training, annual functions.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹47 lakhs have been allocated for holistic student development, including inter-school/college sports and cultural competitions.

projects.name: Smart panels in municipal schools/colleges
projects.description: Rs.1 crore under Smart Education Scheme for smart panels in BNCC schools/colleges.
projects.verbatim_quote: Under the Smart Education Scheme, ₹100 lakhs have been allocated to install smart panels in municipal schools/colleges.

projects.name: Extracurricular sports and arts training (skating, badminton, karate, music, theatre, cricket, swimming)
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh for extracurricular sports/arts training at BNCC schools/colleges; equipment kits.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹50 lakhs have been allocated for training students at all North Bangalore Municipal schools/colleges in extracurricular sports and arts.

projects.name: New schools/colleges in newly constructed buildings
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh to start schools/colleges in newly constructed buildings under BNCC jurisdiction.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹50 lakhs have been allocated to start schools/colleges in newly constructed buildings.

projects.name: School/college library upgrade
projects.description: Rs.50 lakh for upgrading school/college libraries.
projects.verbatim_quote: ₹50 lakhs have been allocated to upgrade school/college libraries.

projects.name: Total Education allocation (2026-27)
projects.description: Rs.23 crore total allocation for education in 2026-27 (includes all education programmes above).
projects.verbatim_quote: A total of ₹23.00 crores has been allocated for education in the 2026-27 fiscal year.
